key_highlights: S&P 500 and Nasdaq post best quarter since 2020, driven by chip stocks and AI optimism.
Alphabet completes record $84.75B equity raise, signaling major AI infrastructure investment.
Supreme Court ruling limits presidential power to fire Fed members, bolstering central bank independence.
Nike declines on cautious demand commentary, while Ford rehires engineers after AI missteps.
Bitcoin slides below $60,000 amid Strategy selloff and regulatory uncertainty.
Gold holds above $4,000 as geopolitical tensions and Fed outlook support safe-haven demand.
Oil falls on Middle East return risks and supply glut concerns.
Comcast announces spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky, driving stock surge.
Chip equipment spending heads toward $250 billion, with Lam Research and KLA benefiting.
Consumer inflation pressures persist, especially for lower-income households and in the South.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum with record quarterly gains; chip equipment spending forecast at $250B.
sectors.implications: AI-driven demand continues to fuel capex; regulatory risks (Taiwan raids Super Micro) and export controls (NVIDIA banned chips) create volatility but also pricing power.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Nike cautious on demand, Live Nation benefits from 'funflation', but lower-income consumers squeezed.
sectors.implications: Divergence between premium experiences and essential goods; inflation disproportionately impacts lower-income households.

sectors.trend: Oil prices falling on Middle East return and supply glut; Chevron secures land for Microsoft's Project Kilby.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risks persist but oversupply fears dominate; clean energy continues to grow as largest new energy source.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan pushes fraud division layoffs despite rising revenues; Bank of America expands sponsorship.
sectors.implications: Cost-cutting in banking amid revenue growth; bond spreads narrow, indicating improving credit conditions.

sectors.trend: Eli Lilly and Regeneron selected for FDA initiative to speed manufacturing reviews.
sectors.implications: Regulatory tailwind for drug manufacturing; positive for biotech and pharma supply chains.

sectors.trend: SAS orders up to 40 Airbus A330s; SpaceX acquisition of Cursor and Rocket Lab buying Iridium.
sectors.implications: Consolidation and expansion in space and aviation; SpaceX joining Nasdaq-100 signals maturing space economy.

sectors.trend: Antipodes Global Fund divested Lennar amid weak housing recovery.
sectors.implications: Housing market recovery remains tepid; interest rate sensitivity persists.

key_themes: AI infrastructure boom driving record equity raises and capex
Central bank independence reinforced by Supreme Court ruling
Consumer divergence: premium experiences thrive, essentials struggle
Geopolitical risks (Middle East, China chip bans) create volatility
Clean energy growth accelerating as largest new energy source
Corporate spin-offs and M&A activity (Comcast, Rocket Lab, SpaceX)
Inflation pressures unevenly distributed across income groups and regions
Chip equipment spending cycle at all-time highs

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across regions; hedge with gold and energy positions; monitor Iran talks and China export controls.
Focus on defensive consumer staples and companies with pricing power; avoid overexposure to discretionary names.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and Legal Uncertainty (Tech, Crypto, Media)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor Supreme Court decisions, SEC actions, and FTC reviews; reduce exposure to highly regulated sectors.
Review insider trading patterns (Vickers data); avoid stocks with unusual insider selling.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Chip Export Controls and Supply Chain Disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in domestic chip equipment makers (LRCX, KLAC) and companies with diversified supply chains.
Limit crypto exposure; use as a small hedge but be prepared for sharp drawdowns.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil Price Volatility and Supply Glut
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce energy sector overweight; focus on integrated majors with strong balance sheets (CVX).

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to semiconductor equipment stocks (LRCX, KLAC) on AI capex cycle.
opportunities.rationale: Chip equipment spending heading toward $250B; BofA raised LRCX target by $150; strong demand from AI and data centers.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Alphabet (GOOGL) on the equity raise dip; use proceeds for AI infrastructure.
opportunities.rationale: Record $84.75B raise signals aggressive AI investment; strong balance sheet and market position.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to gold positions (GLD, IAU) as geopolitical hedge and Fed uncertainty.
opportunities.rationale: Gold holds above $4,000; supported by Iran talks, Fed outlook, and stagflationary risks.
Spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky expected to unlock value; stock surged on news.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in clean energy ETFs (ICLN, TAN) as structural growth theme.
opportunities.rationale: Clean power was world's largest source of new energy in 2025; regulatory tailwinds and cost declines.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary names with weak demand signals (NKE).
defensive_moves.rationale: Nike cautious commentary on demand; lower-income consumer squeezed by inflation; 'funflation' may peak.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price decline with put options on energy ETFs (XLE).
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil falling on supply glut risks; Middle East return could add to oversupply.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce crypto exposure given Bitcoin slide below $60K and Strategy selloff.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bitcoin heading for rare back-to-back quarterly loss; regulatory uncertainty and sell pressure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare, staples).
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation persistence and potential stagflation favor defensive; ECB notes stagflationary energy shock.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets likely to consolidate after strong quarterly gains; focus on Q2 earnings season starting mid-July. Tech sector may see profit-taking after record run, but AI theme remains intact. Bond yields could stabilize as Treasury rally bails out first half. Geopolitical risks (Middle East, China) and inflation data will drive volatility.
market_outlook.long_term: Bullish bias supported by AI infrastructure spending, central bank independence, and clean energy transition. Risks include persistent inflation, consumer weakness, and geopolitical shocks. S&P 500 could test new highs if earnings confirm AI monetization. Watch for regulatory changes in tech and media.
key_catalysts: Fed interest rate decision and dot plot
Student loan policy changes under Trump bill
EU regulatory decisions on Paramount/Warner Bros. deal
Oil supply dynamics and OPEC+ meeting
watch_list: S&P 500 and Nasdaq technical levels after best quarter since 2020
Chip equipment spending data and capex guidance from LRCX, KLAC, AMAT
Bitcoin price action and Strategy's monetization framework
Treasury yield curve and bond spreads
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
UK and EU media ownership reviews
